description_long: This object is a compact, industrial-style shotgun with a cylindrical metal barrel and a robust, utilitarian design. The barrel is thick and appears to be constructed from dark, matte-finished steel, with a slightly wider muzzle and a series of stepped rings along its length. The shotgun features a vertical foregrip made of metal, attached beneath the barrel, and a wooden pistol grip with visible screws, providing ergonomic handling. The trigger is exposed, and there is a small, simple trigger guard integrated into the frame. The overall construction is minimalistic, with no visible stock and a focus on functionality over aesthetics. The combination of metal and wood elements gives it a rugged, makeshift appearance suitable for urban environments.
description: A compact, industrial shotgun with a thick metal barrel, vertical foregrip, and wooden pistol grip, featuring a rugged, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the cylindrical metal barrel, vertical foregrip, and wooden pistol grip with visible screws.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide, circular muzzle and alignment of the foregrip and pistol grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same key features as view 0, with the trigger and grip details visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the pistol grip and the end of the barrel, with the foregrip directly in line.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, cylindrical with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: minimal, small and rounded, closely integrated with the trigger area
